operations.name: agm-analyze
operations.description: Analyze scope, hidden problems, impacts, and trade-offs
operations.deliverable: direct analysis response at light; tracked analysis artifact only at standard/regulated
conditionalInputs: target_kind when repository evidence cannot resolve the affected discipline
operations.example: $agm-analyze depth=light target_files=src/orders.ts objective="Explain duplicate requests; do not edit"
instructions: Inspect the full target and affected callers before conclusions. Database-linked conclusions require actual project code, the applicable db-schema, and representative local examples/data shapes; otherwise label the result preliminary and list the missing evidence without guessing or connecting to a database.

Return CLI-readable plain Markdown in this order: Scope, Evidence, Findings, Impacts, Options, Recommendation, Unknowns. Use short bullets, no wide tables or raw JSON/YAML dumps, and never edit product artifacts.

conditionalReferences.why: backend analysis fundamentals including request-value normalization
project-wide C# shape, naming, boundary, and code examples

conditionalReferences.when: the backend target reads cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device ID
conditionalReferences.why: exact normalize API and compatibility behavior

conditionalReferences.when: FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic is in scope

operations.name: agm-diagnose
operations.description: Diagnose a problem to a proven root cause
operations.deliverable: direct diagnosis response at light; tracked analysis.md only at standard/regulated
conditionalInputs: reproduction evidence when the symptom is not observable from the repository
instructions: Reproduce or trace the symptom, separate causes from correlations, and test bounded hypotheses.
Return the proven root cause, evidence, affected path, and proposed fix; do not apply the fix.

conditionalReferences.why: backend diagnosis fundamentals including request-value normalization
project-wide C# behavior and style baseline

conditionalReferences.when: the backend symptom may involve cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic may contribute to the symptom

operations.name: agm-simulate
operations.description: Simulate scenarios, risks, and observable outcomes
operations.deliverable: direct simulation at light; tracked analysis.md only at standard/regulated
conditionalInputs: inputs and observables when they cannot be derived from the scenario
instructions: Model the initial state, transitions, branches, failure modes, and observables.
Label assumptions and confidence; do not present a simulation as measured runtime fact.

conditionalReferences.why: project-wide C# behavior and style baseline

conditionalReferences.when: the backend scenario involves cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
the scenario includes an application URL, domain, redirect, or callback

operations.name: agm-plan
operations.deliverable: direct plan at light; tracked analysis.md only at standard/regulated
conditionalInputs: decision-owner choice when multiple material approaches remain viable
instructions: Reverse-engineer current behavior, dependencies, callers, tests, and integration order before planning.
Each step names target, action, reason, verification, and decision-owner gate; do not implement.

conditionalReferences.when: the backend plan touches cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
the plan includes FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic

operations.name: agm-design
operations.description: Design FE, BE, SQL, or architecture behavior and acceptance through one product-read-only discipline
operations.deliverable: direct design at light; tracked analysis.md only at standard/regulated
conditionalInputs: target_kind and phase for FE/BE
backend_profile for be-main
decision-owner choice when architecture alternatives are material
operations.example: $agm-design depth=light target=architecture objective="Design ownership and migration for order status"
instructions: Resolve exactly one target discipline before inspection, then define actors, boundaries, data/contract flow, states, transitions, validation, failure/recovery, migration, and measurable acceptance as applicable.
Use only the matching FE, BE, SQL, or architecture references below; remain product-read-only and do not implement.

conditionalReferences.why: project-wide C# contract and boundary examples

conditionalReferences.when: the backend design includes cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution

conditionalReferences.why: SQL schema, result, transaction, and deployment design discipline
canonical service/data ownership and architecture boundaries

conditionalReferences.when: target is FE or BE and URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic is in scope

operations.name: agm-architect
operations.description: Design boundaries, contracts, and migration trade-offs
conditionalInputs: decision-owner approval before an architecture record becomes approved
operations.example: $agm-architect requested_by=Billy objective="Choose ownership boundary for order status"
instructions: Map ownership, contracts, data flow, runtime/deployment effects, alternatives, migration, rollback, and reversible decisions.
Produce a proposed decision record; only recorded authority may approve it.

conditionalReferences.when: the backend architecture includes cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
the architecture includes FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback ownership

operations.name: agm-review
operations.description: Review code or artifacts with evidence-backed findings
operations.deliverable: direct findings at light; tracked analysis.md only at standard/regulated
conditionalInputs: review_scope when the requester wants narrower coverage than the default
instructions: Review correctness, regressions, contracts/data, maintainability, relevant performance, and tests in that order.
Report actionable findings by severity with file/line, evidence, and impact; do not fix them.

conditionalReferences.when: the target is FE, BE, or SQL
conditionalReferences.why: route to the current target pattern only

conditionalReferences.when: the backend review touches cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
the review touches FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic

operations.name: agm-history
operations.description: Query workflow attribution and audit-storage status by person, task, event, or UTC time range
requiredInputs: at least one useful person, task, event, or time-range query
conditionalInputs: explicit UTC offset for timestamp inputs
instructions: Run the workspace history command and answer from versioned workflow evidence, not conversational recall.
Separate requester, execution identity, claimed files, Git authorship, and storage durability.
Remain at light depth: never identify, start, checkpoint, or create task artifacts for a history query.

operations.name: agm-fe
operations.description: Analyze, design, create, edit, refactor, or explicitly test frontend work through one domain façade
operations.deliverable: action-specific frontend analysis, design, bounded implementation, or selected tests
requiredInputs: one action or unambiguous natural-language intent
an objective or pointed frontend target
conditionalInputs: phase and target_kind when repository evidence cannot resolve them
requester selection when embedded test-decision advice finds unspecified coverage
operations.example: $agm-fe action=edit target_files=src/order.component.ts objective="Handle the empty state"

actions.name: analyze
actions.purpose: Inspect frontend scope, behavior, callers, risks, and trade-offs without editing

actions.name: design
actions.purpose: Define frontend flow, states, accessibility, validation, and acceptance without editing

actions.name: create
actions.purpose: Create a bounded new frontend slice

actions.name: edit
actions.purpose: Modify a bounded existing frontend slice

actions.name: refactor
actions.purpose: Refactor frontend code under exactly one behavior mode and a proven preservation boundary

actions.name: test
actions.purpose: Create explicitly requested or selected frontend tests after embedded test-decision advice

instructions: Resolve exactly one action before inspection. Explicit action= wins; otherwise infer only from unambiguous verbs and report the resolved action. Ask one short question when create, edit, refactor, or read versus write remains ambiguous.
For analyze/design, never edit product files. For create/edit/test, show the bounded slice, stay within light limits, and route broader work to agm-prompt before any product write.
For action=refactor, require exactly one mode before editing, preserve the declared logic boundary, and stop with all five modes plus one recommendation when ambiguous.

Apply the embedded test-decision capability during analyze, design, create, edit, and refactor. Classify required, recommended, or not_applicable; write tests only for action=test, a clear test request, or required coverage inside authorized implementation.

conditionalReferences.why: exact refactor behavior and logic-change boundary

conditionalReferences.when: URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic is in scope

operations.name: agm-be
operations.description: Analyze, design, create, edit, refactor, or explicitly test backend work through one domain façade
operations.deliverable: action-specific backend analysis, design, bounded implementation, or selected tests
requiredInputs: one action or unambiguous natural-language intent
an objective or pointed backend target
conditionalInputs: target_kind, backend_profile, and phase when repository evidence cannot resolve them
requester selection when embedded test-decision advice finds unspecified coverage
operations.example: $agm-be action=create target_kind=be-main backend_profile=agmws objective="Add cancel-order endpoint"

actions.name: analyze
actions.purpose: Inspect backend scope, contracts, callers, data, risks, and trade-offs without editing

actions.name: design
actions.purpose: Define backend boundaries, flow, validation, failure behavior, and acceptance without editing

actions.name: create
actions.purpose: Create a bounded new backend slice

actions.name: edit
actions.purpose: Modify a bounded existing backend slice

actions.name: refactor
actions.purpose: Refactor backend code under exactly one behavior mode and a proven preservation boundary

actions.name: test
actions.purpose: Create explicitly requested or selected backend tests after embedded test-decision advice

instructions: Resolve exactly one action before inspection. Explicit action= wins; otherwise infer only from unambiguous verbs and report the resolved action. Ask one short question when create, edit, refactor, or read versus write remains ambiguous.
For analyze/design, never edit product files. For create/edit/test, show the bounded slice, stay within light limits, and route broader work to agm-prompt before any product write.
For action=refactor, require exactly one mode before editing, preserve the declared logic boundary, and stop with all five modes plus one recommendation when ambiguous.

Apply the embedded test-decision capability during analyze, design, create, edit, and refactor. Classify required, recommended, or not_applicable; write tests only for action=test, a clear test request, or required coverage inside authorized implementation.

conditionalReferences.why: exact refactor behavior and logic-change boundary

conditionalReferences.when: the target reads cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device ID
URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic is in scope

operations.name: agm-sql
operations.description: Analyze, design, create, edit, refactor, or explain SQL work through one domain façade
operations.deliverable: action-specific SQL analysis, design, explanation, or bounded SQL implementation
requiredInputs: one action or unambiguous natural-language intent
an objective or pointed SQL target
conditionalInputs: target_kind when table/procedure placement cannot be resolved
output_owner=product|owner-reference|knowledge-draft for new SQL
an exact existing SQL target for edit/refactor
owner evidence for persisted-data, reference promotion, or contract decisions

actions.name: analyze
actions.purpose: Inspect SQL scope, schema, callers, contracts, risks, and trade-offs without editing

actions.name: design
actions.purpose: Define SQL objects, data flow, result and error contracts, deployment, and acceptance without editing

actions.name: create
actions.purpose: Create a bounded new SQL object or slice

actions.name: edit
actions.purpose: Modify a bounded existing SQL object or slice

actions.name: refactor
actions.purpose: Refactor SQL under exactly one behavior mode while preserving declared results, transactions, side effects, errors, and deployment behavior

actions.name: explain
actions.purpose: Explain SQL purpose, contracts, reads/writes, flow, transactions, errors, assumptions, and risks without execution or edits

instructions: Resolve exactly one action before inspection. Explicit action= wins; otherwise infer only from unambiguous verbs and report the resolved action. Ask one short question when create, edit, refactor, or read versus write remains ambiguous.
For analyze/design/explain, never execute SQL, connect to a database, or edit product files. Explain uses FACT, INFERENCE, and UNKNOWN and routes requested modifications to edit.
Edit/refactor requires an exact existing target; otherwise stop SQL_EDIT_TARGET_NOT_FOUND. Never switch to create; preserve the existing path unless migration is authorized.
Create requires output_owner=product|owner-reference|knowledge-draft; otherwise stop SQL_OUTPUT_OWNER_REQUIRED. The absence of a directory is never create authority.
Skill-package meta work never authorizes root product SQL; fixtures/examples require an explicit package-owned path.
For create/edit, show the bounded slice, stay within light limits, load applicable schema/caller evidence, and route broader work to agm-prompt before any product write.

For action=refactor, require exactly one mode before editing; when ambiguous, stop and show all five modes with one recommendation. Preserve result sets, transactions, side effects, error mapping, and deployment idempotency unless recorded authority and the selected mode explicitly allow a change, then format and validate every declared changed SQL path.

references.why: current SQL contract, formatting, and validation

conditionalReferences.why: exact refactor behavior and logic-change boundary

operations.name: agm-qa
operations.description: Verify an artifact under a product-read-only, execution-restricted QA contract
operations.deliverable: direct QA evidence at light without tasks/**; tracked qa.md only at regulated
conditionalInputs: task_id for regulated tracked QA
qa_mode=full only when an explicit full-QA trigger applies
instructions: Default to depth=light and qa_mode=light; a provider/model, SQL/BE/FE target, regulated implementation, or large diff never selects full by itself.

Apply qa-and-done.md, keep product artifacts read-only, and use only read-only inspection plus read-only AgriMap script paths. Never format SQL, install SQLFluff, or connect to LocalDB, dbserver, SQL Server, or another runtime service.

The only product-command exceptions are dotnet build for an existing BE project when compile evidence is necessary, and npm run start:agrimap:development for FE only at qa_mode=full when startup evidence is explicitly necessary; do not run other product commands.
Return only canonical status, evidence, commands actually run, and limitations.

references.why: single QA, correction, and completion policy

conditionalReferences.when: the task is FE, BE, or SQL
conditionalReferences.why: select exactly the matching pattern and Detect gates
backend QA fundamentals including request-value normalization

conditionalReferences.when: BE work reads or refactors cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
conditionalReferences.why: request-value QA behavior matrix for main/library code

conditionalReferences.when: FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic is under verification

operations.name: agm-prompt
operations.description: Analyze and refine requester intent into one immutable versioned Prompt Result with explicit Main and Subagent ownership
operations.deliverable: .agrimap-agent/prompts/YYYY-MM/<conversation-id>/<context>-vNNN.md plus light memory/log/report evidence; never tasks/** or separate role files
conditionalInputs: explicit source Prompt Result path for revision
owner confirmation when implicit source selection is ambiguous
target_kind/backend_profile for target-specific work
operations.example: $agm-prompt objective="Prepare a complete cancel-order implementation prompt" context=cancel-order
instructions: Treat requester input as V0 only. The first finalized model result creates immutable V1; every focused continuation creates the next immutable version without overwriting prior files.

Create exactly one Prompt Package file per version. State Main ownership and either explicit Subagent assignments or explicit none, including files/contracts, forbidden overlap, model profile, verification, and handoff.

Use an explicit source path after validation. Without one, continue only when exactly one credible prompt family exists in the same conversation/context; otherwise stop PROMPT_SOURCE_CONFIRM_REQUIRED.

Remain workflow-write-only and product-read-only. Never create tasks/**, execute the Prompt Result, create separate role instructions, or store model answers/tool output/hidden reasoning as raw history.

references.why: immutable Prompt Result lifecycle, source resolution, and package sections
embedded design support for brief and acceptance

conditionalReferences.when: the Prompt Result defines Subagent assignments
conditionalReferences.why: Main/Subagent ownership, visibility, and integration boundaries
frontend fundamentals that every generated handoff must carry
backend fundamentals that every generated handoff must carry
project-wide C# rules that every generated handoff must carry

conditionalReferences.when: the backend handoff touches cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
conditionalReferences.why: request-value rules that every generated handoff must carry

conditionalReferences.when: target_kind is sql-table, sql-procedure, or sql-table-and-procedure
conditionalReferences.why: SQL fundamentals that every generated handoff must carry

conditionalReferences.when: FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic is in scope

operations.name: agm-exec
operations.description: Execute one decision-owner-approved generated prompt as the execution source of truth with checkpoints, deviation stops, and a Result Package
operations.deliverable: integrated Result Package awaiting canonical QA
requiredInputs: approved prompt path or resumable task_id
conditionalInputs: decision-owner choice when a material deviation exceeds the approved prompt
instructions: Require prompt_status=owner-approved and execute its ordered steps as the task source of truth.
Stop and report structured deviation evidence when a material change exceeds scope; return the Result Package and integration artifact.
Own implementation only: update the acceptance checklist and record integration evidence, but do not create qa.md or result.md; QA and final Leader closure are later phases.

references.why: Prompt Result source of truth and deviation contract
native activity, workspace, and integration contract

conditionalReferences.when: the approved prompt targets C# backend work
the approved prompt targets C# backend work
the approved backend prompt touches cookie, header, query, form, JSON body, or device-ID resolution
the approved Prompt Result touches FE/BE URL, domain, redirect, or callback logic
